title: Ricinophyllum horridum
intro: Ricinophyllum horridum, also known as African ironwood, is a deciduous tree native to South Africa, valued for its medicinal and timber properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Ricinophyllum horridum contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Ricinophyllum horridum is a genus of flowering plants in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to South Africa. The classification of Ricinophyllum horridum places it within the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. Members of the genus are succulent shrubs or small trees, often with thick, waxy stems. Within the Rauvolfioideae subfamily, Ricinophyllum horridum is closely related to other succulent plant genera.
